page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.large is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor from the ARMv9 family, specifically utilizing the Neoverse-V2 model. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs operating at 2.8 GHz, with one thread per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 4.0 GB per core ratio. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate a capable single-core profile but limited multi-core scaling due to the low core count. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the average tier, while multi-core metrics are relatively weak. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression shows strong performance, and passmark database operations register in the average range. However, memory bandwidth write speeds and multi-threaded decompression tasks are weaker.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gn.large offers a dedicated ARM resource profile suitable for lightweight, network-reliant workloads without the overhead of x86 licensing. With a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, it is well-suited for moderate web serving, database operations, and basic utility tasks that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and high network throughput relative to its resource footprint.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.large is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an ARM64-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, highlighted by strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside average database operation speeds, though multi-core scaling and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. This instance offers a cost-efficient, dedicated ARM resource profile for lightweight, network-reliant workloads such as moderate web serving, database operations, and utility tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.large is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for lightweight database and web workloads.

tagline: A dedicated ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring balanced memory density and optimized network bandwidth for lightweight utility and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers average single-core performance and strong single-threaded decompression
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for lightweight database operations, web serving, and utility tasks
